channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — Hawaii's regular sunshine, short rain showers and clean air come together to create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows. They're such a frequent sight that a University of Hawaii professor calls the state the “rainbow capital of the world.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer advance to presidential runoff
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador will choose its next president in a runoff election after conservative incumbent Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González garnered enough votes Sunday to beat 14 other candidates.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mexican long-nosed bats are no strangers to southeastern Arizona. The proof is in the saliva
item.description: FLAGSTAFF, Ariz. (AP) — Scientists have long suspected that Mexican long-nosed bats migrate through southeastern Arizona, but without capturing and measuring the night-flying creatures, proof has been elusive.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and FELICIA FONSECA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ecuador's conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer are leading early presidential voting results
item.description: GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador (AP) — Ecuador is likely to choose its next president in a runoff election later this year as early results of a first-round vote Sunday showed a tight difference between conservative President Daniel Noboa and leftist lawyer Luisa González.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Court grants request to block detained Venezuelan immigrants from being sent to Guantanamo
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — A federal court on Sunday blocked the Trump administration from sending three Venezuelan immigrants held in New Mexico to Guantanamo Bay Naval Base in Cuba as part of the president's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Is the Super Bowl halftime singer lip syncing? Check for these signs
item.description: Casual fans will be watching the theatrics, choreography and guest appearances during Kendrick Lamar and SZA's Super Bowl halftime show. Sleuths will be watching their throats.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official orders consumer protection agency to stop work
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to stop nearly all its work, effectively shutting down an agency that was created to protect consumers after the 2008 financial crisis and subprime mortgage-lending scandal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Remains of all 10 people killed in Alaska plane crash have been recovered: authorities
item.description: The remains of all 10 people killed when their small plane crashed into ice on the Bering Sea have been recovered, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Efforts to put carbon dioxide underground face less support in Trump's second term
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Putting carbon dioxide from power plants and industrial facilities underground where it won’t contribute to global warming could see less federal support and enthusiasm under President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Squatter turns couple's dream home purchase into nightmare
item.description: A New York couple has been unable to move into their new Rochester home because a squatter has taken over and refused to leave, even after payment offers to get him out.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Snow blankets parts of Northeast US, up to a foot expected for some
item.description: A strong storm is expected to bring moderate to heavy snowfall across parts of the Upper Great Lakes on Saturday before intensifying overnight across the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Elon Musk is calling for the impeachment of the federal judge who made a decision early Saturday morning that the Treasury Department should block access to anyone “other than civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties” from its payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk calls for impeachment of judge who blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: The order explicitly prohibits special government employees and those detailed from outside the department from getting access to the systems.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, NewsNation

item.title: DC plane crash victim and Kansas City Chiefs superfan planned return home for family Super Bowl party
item.description: Family and friends confirm a victim of the deadly American Airlines and Army helicopter plane crash was an avid Kansas City Chiefs fan with plans to watch the Super Bowl with his family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 competitive primaries to watch in 2026
item.description: Primary battles are already underway in key Senate and gubernatorial races ahead of the 2026 midterms, an election that will determine not only control of Congress but also the governorships in several states.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WGN 9

item.title: Trump accelerates Guantánamo Bay migrant directive: What to know
item.description: Trump administration officials are rapidly moving forward with President Trump’s directive to turn Guantánamo Bay into a facility that could hold up to 30,000 migrants who are being deported from the United States. Since announcing the move Jan.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: ICE arrests illegal migrant accused of entering the country 6 times
item.description: Jorge Carvajal Castrejon, 36, an illegal migrant, was arrested Jan. 28 in Houston after allegedly entering the US six times, during an enhanced ICE operation with partners from the FBI and other agencies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 suspects, including victim's husband, charged in stabbing death of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee
item.description: Two people were charged in last year's stabbing death of a Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police said, noting that one of the suspects was the victim's husband.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender, vegan 'Zizian' cult linked to Vermont border agent killing dependent on zapping human emotions
item.description: The killing of US Border Patrol agent David Maland in Vermont near the Canadian border appears to be linked to a transgendered cult on the West Coast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Post Malone, Travis Scott, Latto perform at star-studded, invite-only Fanatics Super Bowl party
item.description: Meek Mill took the Fanatics Super Bowl party stage and set the tone: After an energetic performance of “Dreams and Nightmares,” the rapper draped an arm around Michael Rubin and gave him ultimate credit for another stellar event.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce spotted out in New Orleans
item.description: Power couple Taylor Swift and Kansas City Chiefs Tight End Travis Kelce were spotted on Friday night eating at a restaurant while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A$AP Rocky's friend testifies that the rapper fired a prop gun, not a real firearm, in 2021 shooting
item.description: A$AP Rocky's longtime friend and collaborator testified at his Los Angeles trial Friday that the hip-hop star fired a starter pistol that he carried as a prop during a scuffle with a former friend on a Hollywood street in 2021.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ending Kennedy Center board members' terms, naming himself chair in place of Rubenstein
item.description: President Trump on Friday said he would end the terms of multiple members of the Kennedy Center board and make himself chair of a new board, putting himself at the helm of the Washington, D.C., cultural institution.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Judges curb Trump’s sweeping executive actions
item.description: Federal judges are curbing President Trump’s sweeping directives to reshape the government, issuing a flurry of rulings blocking his agenda from charging forward.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Slidell woman arrested in death investigation of Kansas City reporter
item.description: Louisiana law enforcement continue to investigate that death of a Kansas City reporter visiting New Orleans for Super Bowl coverage.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Second type of bird flu found in US dairy cows
item.description: Experts said it raises new questions about wider spread and the difficulty of controlling infections in animals and the people who work closely with them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump doubles down on Gaza Strip idea, says no US soldiers would be needed
item.description: The president in an early Thursday post expanded on his idea, making clear he was serious after some advisers sought to clarify or temper aspects of the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police seek motive after a suspect was arrested for a fatal shooting at an Ohio cosmetics warehouse
item.description: NEW ALBANY, Ohio (AP) — A man accused of shooting to death a coworker and wounding five others at an Ohio warehouse has spoken with police as they probe for a motive, authorities said Wednesday, hours after he was arrested in an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Honolulu records 6th death from massive explosion of illegal fireworks over New Year's
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A sixth person died Wednesday from injuries sustained when crates of illegal fireworks ignited during a New Year’s Eve party in a Honolulu neighborhood, setting off a chain of explosions that left more than a dozen people with severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 arrested in connection to string of high-end burglaries, including one at Joe Burrow's home
item.description: The men have been accused of selling the stolen goods pilfered from "wealthier neighborhoods" and, according to sources, the home of Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WGN 9
